Analysis of Factors Influencing Fire Incident Financial Loss in Toronto

Test: The large difference between MAE and RMSE confirms the instability in large loss fires.

       Metric       Value
0  RMSE (log)       2.410
1   MAE (log)       1.747
2    R2 (log)       0.532
3    RMSE ($)  119814.139
4     MAE ($)   30681.905
5      R2 ($)       0.377

To improve the R2 scores for log dollar and dollar, I Tried two stages models. But it turns out doesn’t help much, likely due to the internal noise.

The performance is even worse, because the classifier misclassifies many true zero-loss cases as non-zero, forcing the regression model to assign them positive predictions. This likely occurs because many “zero-loss” cases in the dataset are not truly zero but reflect reporting issues or missing values, making the classification boundary inherently noisy.

Best XGB: {'colsample_bytree': 1.0, 'learning_rate': 0.05, 'max_depth': 4, 'min_child_weight': 1, 'n_estimators': 400, 'subsample': 0.9} 2.523657680561874
